coredata.dc:title: A FRET immunosensor for sensitive detection of CA 15-3 tumor marker in human serum sample and breast cancer cells using antibody functionalized luminescent carbon-dots and AuNPs-dendrimer aptamer as donor-acceptor pair
coredata.dc:creator: Mohammadi, Somayeh
Salimi, Abdollah
Hamd-Ghadareh, Somayeh
Fathi, Fardin
Soleimani, Farzad
coredata.dc:description: We proposed a FRET immunosensing for detection of CA15-3 tumor marker by highly biospecific interactions between CA 15-3 antigen and the corresponding antibody and aptamer. In this sandwich type immunoassay, CA15-3 antibody-functionalized carbon dots and AuNPs labeled PAMAM-Dendrimer/aptamer were used as donor/acceptor, respectively. When CA 15-3 Ag was added to homogenous immunoassay, the strong complex interaction between CA15-3 Ab-CA15-3 Ag- aptamer caused in more coming closer carbon dot and AuNPs and more decreasing fluorescence signal. The decreased fluorescence intensity was linear at three ranges including in concentration range 1.1 μUmL−1 to 16 μU mL−1 with regression of R2 = 0.9879, at the concentration range 16 μU mL−1 to 0.163 mU mL−1 with regression of R2 = 0.9944 and at the concentration range 0.163 mU mL−1 to 5.0 mU mL−1 with regression of R2 = 0.9805. The detection limit of the FRET immunoassay was 0.9 μU mL−1. This assay revealed good sensitivity and specificity with MDA-MB-231 breast cancer cells concentrations from 1000 to 40000 cells/mL with correlation coefficient of 0.9955 and detection limit of 300 cells/mL (3 cells in 10 μL of injected sample). In addition, this FRET immunosensing is applicable in diluted human serum. The recovery values were in the range of 95.86–96.97% for CA 15-3 Ag in spiked serum sample with RSD lower than 7.3%. The proposed immunoassay could be a valid model for establishing other immunoassays for detection of different cancer tumor markers with relevant antigens and antibodies.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Fluorescence resonance energy transfer
Carbon dot
AuNPs
Aptamer
PAMAM-Dendrimer
CA 15-3 immunoassay
Breast cancer cells
Cancer diagnosis

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Attachment of Ab anti-CA 15-3 to C-dots
Synthesis of AuNPs, and co-immobilization of PAMAM and aptamer onto AuNPs
Optimization of the experimental conditions for CA 15-3 detection
Analytical performance and specificity of immunosensor
Determination of CA15-3 Ag in serum samples
Detection of MDA-MB-231 cell lines with FRET assay

xocs:funding.xocs:funding-agency-matched-string: Iranian Nanotechnology initiative and Research Office of University of Kurdistan
Programming and Management Organization of Kurdistan Province

xocs:funding-list.xocs:funding-text: The authors also thank also the Programming and Management Organization of Kurdistan Province for partly financial support (grant number 4.53053 ). The financial support of Iranian Nanotechnology initiative and Research Office of University of Kurdistan (grant number 4.1261 ) is also gratefully acknowledged.

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: Representative HRTEM image of synthesized AuNPs (A, C), AuNPs conjugated with PAMAM (B, D) and their particle size distribution histogram, Corresponding TEM image of C-dots and the inset presents particle size distribution histogram (E) FT-IR spectra of Histidine and C-dots (F).
The absorption, excitation and emission spectra of prepared C-dots (A) The fluorescence spectra of C-dots scanned at different excitation wavelength (B).
Spectra overlapping between emission of C-dots and absorption spectra of AuNPs and AuNPs/PAMAM/aptamer.

The linear relationship between F0-Fq/F0 and CA 15-3 Ag concentration (A) Fluorescence spectra of FRET immunoassay in the presence of different concentrations of CA 15-3 Ag (B) Fluorescence intensity of FRET immunoassay in the presence of MDA-MB-231 cancer cells at range 1000–40,000 cells (C) The linear relationship between ΔF (F-F0) and the cell line number from 1000 to 40000 (1000, 2000, 3000, 6000, 10000, 20000, 25000, 30000, 40000) (D).

Selectivity of FRET immunoassay; analytical signal of C-dots-Ab/AuNPs-PAMAM-aptamer conjugates after addition of 0.57 U mL−1 of CEA, 3.82 U mL−1 of CA 125, 3.82 U mL−1 of AFP, 0.38 U mL−1 of CA 15-3 and mixture of (CEA, CA 125, AFP and CA 15-3).
Schematic illustration of the FRET immunoassay for CA 15-3 detection.
Comparison of analytical performance of proposed immunoassay with the various procedures for CA15-3 Ag detection.

row.entry: Scanning electrochemical microscopy with enzyme immunoassay
Electrochemical Immunoassay using ferrocenecarboxylic (Fc-COOH)-doped silica nanoparticles (SNPs)
Electrochemical immunosensor with N-doped graphene-modified electrode
Disposable electrochemical immunosensor using peroxidase-like magnetic silica nanoparticles/graphene oxide composites
Nonenzymatic immunosensor based on hierarchical nanoporous PtFe alloy
Sandwich-like electrochemical immunosensor based on hierarchical AuPd nanochain networks
Electrochemical immunosensor based on catalytic activity of cobalt sulfide/graphene nanocomposite
Electrochemiluminescence immunosensor based on Ru(bpy)3 2+ and Ag nanoparticles doped SBA-15
Electrochemiluminescence detection based on immobilizing luminol on dendrimer functionalized ZnO nanorods
Electrochemiluminescence immunosensor based on PAMAM sulfanilic acid-Ru(bpy)3 2+ and PAMAM-CdTe@CdS nanocomposite
ZnO nanorods quartz crystal microbalance (QCM) sensors
FRET immunoassay between modified nitrogen doped carbon dots and gold nanoparticles

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: Calculated recoveries and RSD% of CA 15-3 in spiked samples.

head.ce:title: A FRET immunosensor for sensitive detection of CA 15-3 tumor marker in human serum sample and breast cancer cells using antibody functionalized luminescent carbon-dots and AuNPs-dendrimer aptamer as donor-acceptor pair

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Department of Chemistry, University of Kurdistan, 66177-15175, Sanandaj, Iran

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Research Center for Nanotechnology, University of Kurdistan, 66177-15175, Sanandaj, Iran

ce:affiliation.ce:label: c
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Cellular and Molecular Reserch Center, Kurdistan University of Medical Sciences, 66177-13446 Sananandaj, Iran

ce:correspondence.ce:text: Corresponding author. Department of Chemistry, University of Kurdistan, 66177-15175, Sanandaj, Iran.

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: We proposed a FRET immunosensing for detection of CA15-3 tumor marker by highly biospecific interactions between CA 15-3 antigen and the corresponding antibody and aptamer. In this sandwich type immunoassay, CA15-3 antibody-functionalized carbon dots and AuNPs labeled PAMAM-Dendrimer/aptamer were used as donor/acceptor, respectively. When CA 15-3 Ag was added to homogenous immunoassay, the strong complex interaction between CA15-3 Ab-CA15-3 Ag- aptamer caused in more coming closer carbon dot and AuNPs and more decreasing fluorescence signal. The decreased fluorescence intensity was linear at three ranges including in concentration range 1.1 μUmL−1 to 16 μU mL−1 with regression of R2 = 0.9879, at the concentration range 16 μU mL−1 to 0.163 mU mL−1 with regression of R2 = 0.9944 and at the concentration range 0.163 mU mL−1 to 5.0 mU mL−1 with regression of R2 = 0.9805. The detection limit of the FRET immunoassay was 0.9 μU mL−1. This assay revealed good sensitivity and specificity with MDA-MB-231 breast cancer cells concentrations from 1000 to 40000 cells/mL with correlation coefficient of 0.9955 and detection limit of 300 cells/mL (3 cells in 10 μL of injected sample). In addition, this FRET immunosensing is applicable in diluted human serum. The recovery values were in the range of 95.86–96.97% for CA 15-3 Ag in spiked serum sample with RSD lower than 7.3%. The proposed immunoassay could be a valid model for establishing other immunoassays for detection of different cancer tumor markers with relevant antigens and antibodies.

ce:section.ce:para: With the growing number of cancer cases being diagnosed worldwide, cancer is the second leading cause of death in the World due to late disease detection [1]. Five most common cancers among men are Lung, prostate, colorectal, stomach and liver cancer, while the most common cancers among women are breast, colorectal, lung, cervix and stomach cancer. Breast cancer, the most common cancer among women, impacted over 1.5 million women each year, and the second major cause of cancer-related death in females after lung cancer. In 2015, 570,000 women died from breast cancer – that is approximately 15% of all cancer deaths among women [2,3]. Breast cancer is a complex and heterogeneous diseases and may compose of several subtypes with distinct morphologies and clinical implications [4]. The established risk factors for breast cancer are numerous, including family history of breast cancer, BRCA1 or 2 mutations, some reproductive factors (nulliparity, early age at menarche, late menopause and later age at first full-term pregnancy), alcohol drinking, physical inactivity, excess body weight (postmenopausal breast cancer), the use of exogenous hormones (oral contraceptives and combined postmenopausal hormone replacement therapy), and radiation exposure [5,6]. Currently, early detection and diagnosis of breast cancer is the best means of reducing the mortality and morbidity associated with it because long-term survival rates drop significantly once metastasis has occurred. The identification of disease biomarkers has the clinical significance in detection of tumor cells and they can be detected in a variety of fluids, tissues and cell lines [7–9].

Some breast cancer biomarkers have commonly used in clinical diagnosis, including tissue markers (hormone receptors, human epidermal growth factor-2, urokinase plasminogen activator, plasminogen activator inhibitor, p53 and cathepsin D) and genetic markers (BRAC1 and 2 and gene expression microarray technique, etc.). The serum markers (CA 15-3, CA 27–29, MCA, CA 549, carcino embryonic antigen, oncoproteins, and cytokeratins) are also used in present diagnosis [10] because they play an important role in patient management and the early screening test. CA15-3 with much better clinical specificity than CEA is the most widely used serum marker in breast cancer. Patients with primary breast cancer or metastatic breast cancer show increased CA15-3 to more than 30 units/mL [11]. Consequently, the development of simple (in comparison to ELISA [12], Western-blot, etc.), cost-effective biosensing devices able to rapidly detect CA15-3 human blood at low levels as tools complementary to those traditional methods to improve the effectiveness of breast cancer detection and the follow-up of breast cancer patients would be of great interest. In the last years, several sensing platforms for the determination of CA15-3 levels were developed, including electrochemical immunosensor [13–21], electrochemiluminescence immunosensor [22–24], Lectin-based optical sensing [25], quartz crystal microbalance [26], silicon-based ultrasonic immunoassay [27] and Optofluidic Ring Resonator Sensors [11].

Recently, a growing interest has been emerged in developing cancer immunoassays for biomedical applications, because, they can measure very low levels of biomarkers in physiological samples which can help in the diagnosis of cancer at an early stage [28]. Usually, the heterogeneous and homogeneous immunoassays are two common types immunoassay technique [29]. Homogeneous immunoassays, involving the immobilization of the biomolecules on the nanomaterials, take place in the solution. The homogeneous immunoassay can be used for the detection of complex samples, such as urine or blood, without the large sample consumption, sample pretreatment and separation of the bound and unbound label, resulting in a relatively inexpensive and easy performance [30].

Fluorescence immunoassay is a commonly used optical biosensor that has gained particular attentions in medicine and nanotechnology recently owing to have the advantage of fluorescence technique (high sensitivity) and the immunoassay (high selectivity). Fluorescence Resonance Energy Transfer (FRET), a non-radiative physical phenomenon, is being used more and more in biomedical research today and the efficiency of the energy transfer process is dependent on close proximity and suitable alignment between the donor and acceptor as well as a dipole-dipole interaction [31].

The emerging fields of nanotechnology open a new horizon for nanomaterials applications as fluorophore and quencher in FRET system. The application of carbon dots in FRET-based biosensing provides many advantages for the challenging diagnostic requirement, including strong, stable, and tunable fluorescence emission, excellent photostability, favorable biocompatibility, low toxicity (compared with traditional semiconductor and noble metal NPs, do not contain any heavy metal cations (Cd2+, Cu2+, Hg2+, Au3+, Ag+) and toxic anions (Te2−, Se2−, etc.)), and good water solubility [32]. The use of gold nanoparticles (Au NPs) as highly efficient fluorescence quenchers (acceptors) in FRET-based biosensing have been demonstrated recently [33–35], due to high surface-area-to volume ratio, significant surface plasmon resonance, tunable dimensions and ease of functionalization [36]. Gold–sulfur binding is the most popular and effective mechanism for functionalization of AuNPs [37]. Furthermore, PAMAM dendrimers as monodisperse and highly branched macromolecules with active functional groups can be used to immobilize antibodies or aptamers that can lead to increase the sensitivity of immunoassays [38].

Among the newest tools in the fight against cancer, aptamers have gained increasing research interest. They can bind to specific targets like proteins, peptides, organic molecules, metal ions and even entire cells with high affinity and specificity [39]. In comparison to other targeting agents, aptamers have several distinguished advantages, such as ease of synthesis, stability in harsh biological environments, small size, ease of conjugating to nanomedicine-based platforms, require no complicated and expensive purification steps, and high affinity to targets, so they can be used as suitable molecular probes for the sensitive detection disease biomarkers and cancer cells in low concentration [40].

Herein, a selective FRET-immunoassay was proposed for sensitive detection of CA 15-3 tumor marker based on highly specific interactions between CA 15-3 antigen and the corresponding antibody and aptamer. In this sandwich type optical immunoassay, CA 15-3 antibody-functionalized carbon dots were used as donor fluorescence and AuNPs labeled PAMAM-Dendrimer/aptamer were used as quencher. For increasing fluorescence quenching ability of AuNPs, PAMAM-Dendrimer applied for conjugation of AuNPs surface which provided possibility of more aptamers attachment and exhibited low detection limit. When CA 15-3 Ag was added to homogenous immunoassay, the strong complex interaction between CA15-3 Ab-CA15-3 Ag- aptamer caused in more coming closer carbon dot and AuNPs and more decreasing fluorescence signal. The proposed immunoassay was used for CA15-3 detection in serum samples with satisfactory results. Also, this immunoassay was applied to the assay of CA 15-3 extracts from breast cancer cells in MDA-MB-231 cell lines. It is expected that the proposed immunoassay could be a valid model for establishing other immunoassays for detection of different cancer tumor markers with relevant antigens and antibodies.

The CA15-3, CEA, AFP, CA125 EIA Kit was purchased from CanAg Diagnostics (Gothenburg, Sweden), and CA15-3 EIA Kit consisted of CA15-3 standards (containing 0, 15, 50, 125, and 250 U mL−1) and biotin anti-CA-15-3 monoclonal antibody from mouse approximately 2.5 μg mL−1 contains phosphate buffered saline (pH 7.2) with bovine serum albumin. Polyamidoamine dendrimer (PAMAM) (generation 4, G4) were purchased from Sigma and Aldrich (Sigma-Aldrich, USA). Tetrachloroauric acid (HAuCl4), and all other reagents with analytical grade were from Merck (Merck, Germany) or Fluka. 1-Ethyl-3-(3-dimethylaminopropyl) carbodiimide hydrochloride (EDC), N-hydroxy succinimide (NHS), was bought from Abcam Company (Cambridge, UK). Dialysis bags (molecular weight cut off = 1000 Da) and glutaraldehyde were also ordered from Sigma-Aldrich (Sigma-Aldrich, USA). Phosphate buffer solution with pH 7.4 was used for all the measurements. The 5′-amine-modified aptamer having the sequence 5′-GAAGTGAATATGACAGATCACAACT was synthesized by BIONEER (Bioneer, South Korea), and purified using HPLC. The instruments applied for absorption, fluorescence emission and nanomaterial characterization reported in supplementary information (SI) file.

Ultraviolet–visible (UV–vis) absorption spectra (200–800 nm) were recorded by UV–vis spectroscopy (Varian Cary 5000 UV–vis–NIR absorption spectrometer, Agilent Technologies, USA), utilizing quartz cuvettes with optical path lengths of 1 Cm. Fluorescence spectra were recorded using Varian Cary Eclipse Fluorescence Spectrophotometer (Agilent, USA) at different excitation/measurement wavelengths. The excitation slit widths were 10 nm and emission slit widths were 10 nm, and the detector applied voltage was 540 V. FTIR spectra, TEM images were used for characterization of AuNPs and C-dots. Transmission electron microscopy (TEM) was performed with a FEI Tecnai microscope operated at 200 kV.

C-dots were synthesized using hydrothermal treatment of histidine following the procedure described in the literature [41]. Briefly, 1.0 g histidine was added to 25 mL NaOH (0.5 mol L−1). Then the obtained solution was transferred to a 50 mL Teflon equipped stainless steel auto-clave (50 mL), which was heated at 180 °C for 12 h. The large dots removed through centrifugation and then the collected C-dots were dialyzed against ultra-pure water through a dialysis membrane (molecular weight cut-off = 1000) for 48 h. The prepared C-dots were kept at room temperature for further characterization and use.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Attachment of Ab anti-CA 15-3 to C-dots
ce:section.ce:para: For this sandwich type optical immunoassay, the biotin anti-CA-15-3 monoclonal antibody covalently attached to carbon dots using EDC/NHS as activating agents. The surface carboxylic groups of C-dots were activated in the presence of EDC/NHS. Briefly, EDC and NHS solution (100 mmol/L, 30 μL) were added to 100 μL of the carboxylate-modified C-dot solution (0.75 mg/mL). After incubation at 4 °C for 1 h, 100 μL of antibody solution (0.25 μg mL−1 in 0.01 M PBS) and 740 μL of deionized water were added to this mixture and the prepared C-dots-Ab incubated in the refrigerator at 4 °C before use.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Synthesis of AuNPs, and co-immobilization of PAMAM and aptamer onto AuNPs
ce:section.ce:para: Citrate coated AuNPs were synthesized according to a modified version of the previous reported method [42]. Synthesize of thiol terminated PAMAM dendrimer and functionalization of AuNPs with thiolated PAMAM dendrimer was performed according to a published report with a minor modification [38]. C5 amine Aptamer covalently attached to AuNPs-PAMAM by glutaraldehyde as linker. Aptamer attachment onto AuNPs-PAMAM was via reaction between NH2 group in PAMAM and NH2 groups in C5 amine modified Aptamer. The related detail reported in SI.

The human breast cancer cell line MDA-MB-231 obtained from the national cell bank of the Iran (Pasteur institute of Iran, Tehran). MDA-MB-231 cell lines were cultured in Dulbecco's modified Eagle's medium (D- MEM) supplemented with 10% fetal bovine serum (FBS; Invitrogen) and 1% penicillin/streptomycin (Invitrogen) in a 5% CO2 atmosphere at 37 °C.

A sandwich type fluorescence immunoassay based on energy transfer between C-dots and Aptamer/AuNPs was performed as follows: The predetermined amount of N,C-dots (final concentration 0.176 mg/mL) were mixed with the different amounts of Aptamer/AuNPs in 10 mM PBS (pH 7.4). All fluorescence spectra were recorded with an excitation wavelength of 350 nm and under emission wavelength of 445 nm. After that, different amounts of CA 15-3 Ag (0–0.005 U mL−1) were added and mixed, and the fluorescence spectra were measured. In order to investigate the specifity of the immunoassay, CEA, AFP and CA-125 were tested as possible interference substances. All experiments data is obtained by taking the average of three replicates.

TEM images were used to characterize the morphology and size of AuNPs with or without modifications, as clearly shown in Fig. 1 A,B,C and D. Modified and unmodified AuNPs appeared as sphere and well-dispersed. According to size distribution diagram (inset Fig. 1A and B), the average particle size of the unmodified AuNPs was 15 nm, whereas PAMAM modified AuNPs increased to the average size of 28 nm, confirming AuNPs modifications.

The formation of thiol terminated PAMAM was examined by comparison of FT-IR spectra of PAMAM and thiol-terminated PAMAM. As can be seen in Fig. S1, appearance of new bands at 2558 cm−1 confirmed the attachment of thiol groups to PAMAM [33]. Further characterization was done using UV–visible absorption spectroscopy to evaluate conjugation of PAMAM onto the AuNPs surface. As shown in Fig. S2, the surface plasmon resonance absorbance peak of the AuNPs revealed at 521 nm. After attaching PAMAM molecules to the surface of the AuNPs, the absorption peak of AuNPs-PAMAM slightly shifted from 521 nm to 527 nm. These results shown AuNPs surface were successfully modified by PAMAM. TEM images and size distribution of C-dots are shown in Fig. 1E. The C-dots are observed to be well-dispersed and monodispersed. The inset of Fig. 1E showed the size distribution ranges of C-dots are from 1.0 to 8.0 nm and most particles diameters are 3.0 nm.

The FTIR spectrum of the N,C-dots in Fig. 1F also was utilized to further confirm the chemical structure of the synthesized C-dots. As shown in Fig. 1F, the presence of amide groups can be demonstrated by the stretching vibrations of amide I, C=O, amide II, N−H, and amide III, CN centered at 1609, 1492, and 1384 cm−1, respectively. The broad bands centered at 3401, 3231, 3130 cm−1, and the peak at 1088 cm−1 were attributed to –OH, –NH2, –NH3 + and CO.

Optical properties of the synthesized C-dots are often applied for the characterization of C-dots and the obtained results are shown in Fig. 2 . In the UV–vis absorption spectrum (Fig. 2A), the C-dots indicated optical absorption in the UV region (230–320 nm), with a tail extending into the visible region. The peak at 235 nm can be arose from the π−π* transition of C=N or C=C bonds, whereas a shoulder at 300 nm can be attributed to the n−π* transition of the carbonyl or amine groups on the surface of N,C-dots [43]. The obtained results in Fig. 2A indicated that C-dots emit intense blue light with the emission band around 450 nm under exciting at 350 nm. When the N,C-dots were excited at a range of wavelengths from 300 to 450 nm, the corresponding emission peaks shifted from 423 to 513 nm (Fig. 2B). According to FTIR results, there are multiple functional groups containing C-, N- and O on the surface of the synthesized C-dots which causes various surface states with different energy levels and thus a series of emissive traps. So, such excitation-dependent fluorescent behavior was related to the different surface states of C-dots surfaces [44,45].

To ensure the feasibility of FRET from carbon dots to AuNPs, the absorption and emission spectra of modified AuNPS and C-dots have measured. As shown in Fig. 3 , the desirable spectral overlapping between emission spectra of C-dots and absorption spectra of modified AuNPs is observed, confirming that fluorescence energy transfer from C-dot to modified AuNP could occur if they were spatially close together.

Gold nanoparticles (NPs) are the most commonly applied as quenching acceptors in FRET based applications, often due to their unique properties, such as plasmon resonances in the visible range (typically with high molar extinction coefficients 1 × 109 M−1cm−1 for d = 20 nm AuNP), stable, unfluctuating signal intensities, and resistance to photobleaching. Various size gold NPs can be synthesized by the citrate-reduction procedure (diameter 16–147 nm) [46]. Gold NPs have been used successfully in FRET applications as quenchers for fluorescent dye [34], semiconductor QDs [47] and carbon dots (C-dots) [48] for the sensing of cardiac troponin T (cTnT), carcinoembryonic antigen (CEA), aflatoxin B1, respectively. Recently, Hamd-Ghadareh and co-workers reported an ultrasensitive antibody-ssDNA aptamer sandwich-type fluorescence immunosensor for CA125 determination. They used AuNPs labeled PAMAM-Dendrimer/Ab as quencher [33]. Dendrimers are highly-branched polymers that can be conjugated with other chemical functionalities or adsorbed on the external shell of metal nanoparticles. Since the AuNPs surface is stabilized with poly(amidoamine) dendrimers (PAMAM), the free amines on the dendrimer could be used in bioconjugation. Agnihotri and coworkers used modified SELEX methodology for selecting DNA aptamers against breast tumor marker (CA 15-3). The GAAGTGAATATGACAGATCACAACT sequence with KD 45.47 ± 3.415 (nM) were selected because this DNA aptamer has more binding affinity against CA 15-3 [49]. In this study, AuNPs/PAMAM linked to breast cancer aptamer with the help of homofunctional linkages (glutaraldehyde) applied as quencher. Good spectral overlapping between the emission spectra of the donor and the absorption spectra of the acceptor, the distance between the donor and the acceptor and the orientation of the donor binding onto the acceptor are very important for efficient FRET between donor and acceptor. Fig. 3 indicted that the UV–vis absorption of AuNPs and AuNPs/PAMAM/aptamer have good spectral overlap with the normalized emission spectra of C-dots (donor).

The sensing mechanism of this sandwich type immunoassay for CA 15-3 detection indicated in Scheme 1 . The sensing mechanism contains following steps: (a) Basically, richness of –COOH groups on the surface of carbon dots provided a suitable basis for surface modification. Carboxyl groups on C-dots surface activated by EDC/NHS for attaching of Ab on C-dots surface (C-dots-Ab). (b) Then, AuNPs modified in two steps: modification of AuNPs with thiol terminated PAMAM through covalent bonding Au−S; then, immobilization of C5 amine-aptamer onto PAMAM-AuNPs by glutaraldehyde as linker. (c) Addition of AuNPs/PAMAM/aptamer to C-dots-Ab led to decrease in fluorescence intensity due to FRET from C-dots-Ab to AuNPs/PAMAM/aptamer. (d) In the presence of CA 15-3 Ag, biospecific interaction between CA 15-3 Ag and corresponding Ab and aptamer, brought C-dots and AuNPs in closer. As a result, the FRET process occurs more and the fluorescence intensity decreases further. By measuring the fluorescence intensity change of the solution, CA 15-3 can be detected.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Optimization of the experimental conditions for CA 15-3 detection
ce:section.ce:para: The experimental conditions for immunoreaction including C-dots concentration, concentration of PAMAM-AuNPs labeled CA 15-3 aptamer and incubation time have a significant effect on the optical response of immunosensing assay. It is important to choose the proper concentration of C-dots. As shown in Figs. S3 and S4, the optimized concentration of C-dots and AuNPs in our immunoassay were obtained 0.176 mg/mL and 0.236 nM, respectively. Then, for investigation of the catalytic behavior of modified PAMAM-AuNPs, the volume ratio of PAMAM-AuNPs was optimized. The fluorescence intensity of C-dots-Ab decreased gradually and reached a minimum amount at a dilution ratio of 1:100 of PAMAM-AuNPs. The stability of the C-dots was also investigated at different pH at the range of 2.9–7.4. As illustrated in Fig. S5, in acidic conditions (pH 2.9 to3.45), fluorescence of C-dots was high. It is deduced that the amino groups of C-dots can be protonated gradually as the pH value decreases and thus the possible proton transfer from the protonated amino groups to the conjugated carbon structure enhances the fluorescence of the carbon dots [50,51]. The fluorescence intensity is almost constant from 4.6 to 7.4. Thus, biological pH (7.4) was selected in the later sensing processe.

The incubation times between C-dots-Ab and AuNPs-PAMAM-aptamer in the range of 0–50 min with interval of 5 min was investigated. The obtained results in Fig. S6 showed that the fluorescence intensity of C-dots-Ab was obviously quenched by AuNPs-PAMAM-aptamer in 5 min and almost remained constant in the next 45 min. To obtain stable signal, the time period of 10 min was selected as the optimized incubation time for C-dots-Ab and AuNPs-PAMAM-aptamer without target.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Analytical performance and specificity of immunosensor
ce:section.ce:para: Based on done research, this platform was explored as a sensitive immunoassay for CA 15-3 detection. In order to investigate the efficiency of FRET biosensor, different concentrations of CA 15-3 Ag were tested from 0.000001 U mL−1 to 0.005 U mL−1. As illustrated in Fig. 4 , the fluorescence intensity of C-dots-Ab decreased with the addition of AuNPs/PAMAM/aptamer and then decreased more with addition of different concentration of CA 15-3 Ag, which was as a result of formation of C-dots-Ab–Ag–AuNPs/PAMAM/aptamer immunocomplex and FRET process. C-dots were brought close to AuNPs surface and thus fluorescence quenching was occurred.

The plot fluorescence signal change ΔFr ((F0 − Fq)/F0) as a function of the CA 15-3 Ag concentration can be seen in Fig. 4 (A,B), where F0 is the fluorescence intensity of the C-dots-Ab–AuNPs/PAMAM/aptamer in the absence of CA 15-3 Ag, and Fq is the corresponding fluorescence intensity after addition of different concentrations of CA 15-3 Ag. As indicated the decreased fluorescence intensity was linear at three ranges including at the concentration range 1.1 μU mL−1 to 16 μU mL−1 with regression of R2 = 0.9879, at the concentration range 16 μU mL−1 to 0.163 mU mL−1 with regression of R2 = 0.9944 and at the concentration range 0.163 mU mL−1 to 5.0 mU mL−1 with regression of R2 = 0.9805. The calculated detection limit of the FRET immunoassay was 0.9 μU mL−1 for CA 15-3 Ag. This propsed FRET immunoassay, without using amplification techniques, is a sensitive method for CA 15-3 Ag detection. As can be seen in Table 1 , the detection technique, and characteristics such as dynamic range of the corresponding calibration graph, limit of detection achieved, has been compared with some of recently reported immunoassay and electrochemical procedures.

To explore the specificity of proposed fluorescence immunoassay, fluorescence signal changes of CEA, CA 125, AFP and mixture of Ags were compared with fluorescence signal changes of CA 15-3 Ag. As revealed in Fig. 5 , under optimum conditions, the response of FRET immunoassay for 0.38 U mL−1 CA 15-3 Ag, 0.57 U mL−1 CEA, 3.82 U mL−1 CA 125, 3.82 U mL−1 AFP and mixture of Ags (CEA, CA 125, CA 15-3 and AFP) was obtained. The C-dots-Ab/AuNPs-PAMAM-aptamer immunoassay indicates satisfying specificity for CA 15-3 against the other tumor markers. The selectivity of immunosensor could be originated from specific interaction of CA 15-3 Ag to corresponding DNA aptamer and Ab.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Determination of CA15-3 Ag in serum samples
ce:section.ce:para: In order to investigate of the clinical applicability of the fluorescence assay, we performed CA 15-3 analyzing in two diluted serum samples. One of serum sample belonged to patient female person, while the measured CA 15-3 Ag value by the enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA) kit, as a conventional clinical method for the analysis of CA 15-3, was 629.2 U mL−1. The obtained result of determination of CA 15-3 Ag by the proposed immunoassay (with consideration of the dilution factors of serum sample) was 618.39 ± 5.0 U mL−1. The obtained concentration of CA 15-3 Ag in serum patient sample was in good agreement with reported value based on ELISA method in clinical laboratory (629.2 U mL−1). Furthermore, to further investigate the application of the proposed assay for determination of CA 15-3, healthy human serum sample was spiked with two concentrations of CA 15-3 (1.1 mU mL−1 and 2.3 mU mL−1) (Table 2 ). The recovery values were in the range of 96.97–95.86% for CA 15-3 Ag in spiked serum sample with RSD lower than 7.3%. These results proved that nonspecific interaction and cross-reactivity in CA15-3 determination did not distinctly interfere with the immunoassay response. Also, these results demonstrated that our immunoassay is a robust tool for determination of CA 15-3 in clinical purpose and could be used for other analytes by varing aptamer and Ab.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Detection of MDA-MB-231 cell lines with FRET assay
ce:section.ce:para: To investigate the potential applications of suggested immunoassay in biomedical fields, human breast cancer MDA-MB-231 cell lines were selected. The extracts from MDA-MB-231 cell lines of breast cancer, with different cell numbers; 1000, 2000, 3000, 6000, 10000, 20000, 25000, 30000, 35000 and 40000 was analyzed by the presented assay method. As observed in Fig. 4 C,D, the fluorescence signal of the assay had a linear relation with the number of MDA-MB-231 cell concentrations ranging from 1.0 × 103 to 4.0 × 104 cells/mL, with regression equation of ΔF = 0.0175 C-11.983 (R2 = 0.9955) and the calculated detection limit was estimated to be 300 cells/mL (3 cells in 10 μL of injected sample) at a S/N of 3.

A sensitive and efficient nanoparticle based bioassay to detection of biomarkers in physiological samples could replace ELISA; or, they could enhance the performance of existing technologies. Traditional fluorophores such as FAM, Texas red, and Cy5 are not suitable for reliable and long term detection due to high cost and photobleaching effect. The aim of the present study was to develop a FRET immunoassay for CA 15-3 Ag detection in the diagnosis of breast cancer. Fluorescence intensity of C-dots-Ab was quenched by addition of AuNPs-PAMAM-aptamer. Then, the strong complex interaction between CA15-3 Ab-CA15-3 Ag- aptamer can occur in the presence of CA 15-3 Ag which results in more coming closer carbon dot and AuNPs. Therefore, strong FRET between C-dot and AuNPs led to decreased fluorescence intensity. The limit of detection of immunoassay was 0.9 μU mL−1. Furthermore, the suggested immunoassay had potential for detection of MDA-MB-231 cancer cells at range 1000–40,000 cells with detection limit 3 cells/10 μL. According to obtained results, this sensitive immunoassay demonstrated good robustness and specificity in the analysis of serum samples and cancer cells. This fluorescence immunoassay system had several advantages, such as simple operation, fast detection, low cost and no require for special or expensive equipment. This method could be extended to determination of various types of biomarker.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: The authors also thank also the Programming and Management Organization of Kurdistan Province for partly financial support (grant number 4.53053). The financial support of Iranian Nanotechnology initiative and Research Office of University of Kurdistan (grant number 4.1261) is also gratefully acknowledged.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para:
The following is the supplementary data related to this article:
Multimedia component 1 Multimedia component 1

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data related to this article can be found at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ab.2018.06.008.
